scene_summary: A corporate meeting where leadership tries to spin February's underperformance as a 'strategic acceleration opportunity' while junior staff nervously second-guess their own analysis.

observations.likely_role: VP/Head of Operations (the one who owns the numbers)
signature_lines: Before we dive into our March roadmap, I did want to surface that February closed just below our projected performance forecast models.
Right, so become more- Surely, surely.
observations.interpersonal_note: speaks first and most often; sets the tone for the whole meeting; others defer to them

observations.likely_role: middle manager / analyst (the one who validates leadership's framing)
signature_lines: Yeah, go ahead. We look at February attainment from a sort of achievement perspective.
And I think go ahead, getting that here is that we effectively were affected by February shrunk in chronological architecture, right? So now the issue- Oh, absolutely.
observations.interpersonal_note: immediately agrees with and amplifies the cold-boss's points; never contradicts anyone

observations.likely_role: junior analyst / data person (the one who tries to be helpful but keeps getting talked over)
signature_lines: And I think what we should take a look at is does the under-convergence of the February benchmark offer a strategic acceleration opportunity for March? Right.
observations.interpersonal_note: tries to contribute thoughtful analysis but is consistently interrupted or cut off by others; their insights are only acknowledged after they've been dismissed
